sn6.7:1.1:
1
At Sāvatthī. Now on that occasion the Blessed One had gone for his day's abiding and was in seclusion. Then the independent brahmas Subrahma and suddhāvasā approached the Blessed One and stood one at each doorpost. Then, referring to the bhikkhu Kokalika, the independent brahma Subrahma recited this verse in the presence of the Blessed One:
sn6.7:1.2:
2
"What wise man here would seek to define
An immeasurable one by taking his measure?
He who would measure an immeasurable one
Must be, I think, an obstructed worldling.ʺ
